needsMoreLED: Replace remaining bulbs with LEDs and switch off lights in empty rooms.
goodCoverage: Your lighting setup is strong; keep lights only on in occupied spaces.

multiple: Multiple refrigerators can add cost; keep only active units plugged in and set temperatures efficiently.
single: Maintain one fridge by keeping it well sealed and defrosted to save energy.
none: No refrigerators detected; ensure your cooling equipment, if any, is used efficiently.

waterMotors: Run water motors only when necessary and repair leaks to reduce pump runtime.
fans: Use fans in occupied rooms only and turn them off when not needed.

limited: With limited grid supply, use low-power devices and backup lighting during outages.
available: When grid power is available, shift heavy appliance use to daytime or off-peak hours.

powerCuts: During frequent power cuts, unplug idle electronics to protect them and reduce phantom loads.
household: Coordinate appliance use across the household to avoid running many devices simultaneously.

low: Prioritize low-cost savings like turning off unused lights, fans, and chargers.
high: Consider investing in efficient appliances, LED lighting, and smart power strips.

unusedRooms: Close doors to unused rooms and focus lighting and fans only in occupied areas.

highUsage: Limit online streaming and reduce energy draw by using energy-efficient devices and lower screen brightness.
activeUse: Power down routers and online devices when not in use to cut standby energy use.
unused: Keep online devices off when internet activity is not needed to save energy.

fallback: Review device use and switch off appliances when not needed to lower the current bill.
